title: In Brazil's 'slaughterhouse capital,' workers are under pressure: 'The companies want us to be robots'
subhed: To cope with the demand for meat, expected to increase under the EU-Mercosur agreement, slaughterhouses recruit from among Indigenous populations and immigrants. Employees report harsh working conditions, including a grueling pace, cold temperatures and faulty machines.

title: One year from presidential election, a 'quiet anger' in France's streets

Ukrainian drones strike Russian oil facilities in the Urals after targeting Black Sea coast
subhed: Drone attacks carried out on Wednesday and Thursday targeted a major refinery in Perm, 1,500 kilometers from Ukraine. These attacks further limit Russia's crude oil processing capacity, a key sector of the Kremlin's wartime economy.

title: Who is Pierre-André Imbert, Macron's new chief of staff?
subhed: This senior civil servant, who has extensive experience negotiating with labor unions, was involved in labor reforms under presidents François Hollande and Emmanuel Macron.

title: Seafood and veggie tide inundates dining tables on the Basque coast
subhed: Young talents focused on vegetables and fish have elevated the gastronomic offerings in Biarritz and its neighboring town Bidart, in a region traditionally passionate about meat.

title: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
subhed: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhed: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.

title: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
subhed: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.

title: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
subhed: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.
